reasoning: The short-term estimate is anchored on the FRED statistical tool which projects a recent downward trend (P=0.29) as a strong prior for the near term. The 3-month yield is currently anchored by the Fed Funds rate, and search confirms the Fed is widely expected to hold rates steady at their March 2025 meeting due to sticky inflation risks, keeping short-term yields elevated and resisting spread increases in the immediate future. Over longer horizons, the Fed is projected to eventually cut rates, which would mechanically lower the 3-month yield and tend to push the spread upward (un-inverting the yield curve). Following the instruction to increase uncertainty and approach 0.5 for distant forecasts, the probabilities are graded smoothly from the 0.29 short-term anchor up to 0.49 for the 1-year horizon.
